dimensionDescriptions.ai_enabled_social_engineering: Explain how AI-enhanced social engineering (for example spearphish personalization, deepfake voice, or synthetic identity signals) changed attacker credibility, speed, or scale in this scenario, and what that means for strategic risk.
dimensionDescriptions.iam_saas_risk: Assess identity and SaaS control exposure (M365/IAM settings, device trust assumptions, conditional access, OAuth consent and token governance), and describe how these controls influenced attacker opportunity.
dimensionDescriptions.governance_controls: Provide at least one concrete governance/control recommendation tied to policy, approvals, privileged access management, workforce training, or vendor/third-party controls; include owner and implementation horizon.
dimensionDescriptions.strategic_detection_gap: Identify the highest-impact monitoring, telemetry-retention, or workflow blind spot that limits strategic decision quality, and explain why this gap materially increases institutional risk.
dimensionDescriptions.business_impact: Translate technical findings into mission/business consequences, including effects on treasury/payroll/partner operations, contractual exposure, and potential regulatory or oversight implications.
dimensionDescriptions.defensive_prioritization: Present a prioritized 90-day defense plan with sequencing logic (what happens first, second, third), emphasizing highest risk reduction under realistic staffing and dependency constraints.
dimensionDescriptions.tradeoff: State at least one explicit security-versus-usability/operations tradeoff in your recommended plan, and explain how you will mitigate adoption friction or service disruption.
dimensionDescriptions.stakeholder_communication: Write executive-ready rationale in plain language: what decision is needed, why now, expected impact, and how technical details support—not obscure—the recommendation.
dimensionDescriptions.risk_reasoning: Justify risk using explicit impact × likelihood reasoning tied to scenario evidence, not indicator volume alone; show why your selected risk level is defensible.
dimensionDescriptions.confidence_reasoning: Justify confidence by referencing evidence quality, corroboration, and unresolved ambiguity; explicitly state why confidence is not one level higher or lower.
dimensionDescriptions.uncertainty_collection: Name the highest-yield evidence or validation actions that would reduce uncertainty fastest, and describe exactly how each possible result would change your recommendation.

reportSections.title: Executive Summary (strategic risk statement + recommendation)
reportSections.template: Provide a plain-language strategic risk statement for leadership, identify the decision requiring approval now, and summarize your recommended action path in 2–4 sentences.

reportSections.title: Threat Trend and Scenario Interpretation (AI capability/speed/scale)
reportSections.template: Explain how AI-enabled behaviors in this scenario change attacker capability, speed, or scale versus traditional BEC/identity abuse, and why that shift matters for institutional defenses.

reportSections.title: Evidence and Assumptions (what you can and cannot infer)
reportSections.template: Separate confirmed evidence from assumptions, identify telemetry limits, and note which unknowns most constrain attribution, objective certainty, or response confidence.

reportSections.title: Primary Hypothesis and Alternatives (most defensible interpretation)
reportSections.template: State your primary hypothesis, provide at least one plausible alternative, and justify why your primary interpretation is currently the most defensible given available evidence.

reportSections.title: Risk Assessment (business impact + regulatory/operational implications)
reportSections.template: Assess impact and likelihood explicitly, then connect likely outcomes to mission-critical services, financial control obligations, and operational continuity risk.

reportSections.title: Strategic Detection Gap (what you cannot see and why it matters)
reportSections.template: Identify the largest current monitoring/retention blind spot, explain how it weakens strategic decision-making, and define the most practical fix.

reportSections.title: Governance and Control Recommendations (90-day plan, sequencing, owners)
reportSections.template: Recommend sequenced governance/control actions across 90 days, assign accountable owners, and indicate dependencies or policy approvals needed for execution.

reportSections.title: Tradeoffs and Change Management (usability/operations + adoption risk)
reportSections.template: Describe operational/usability tradeoffs of your plan, likely resistance points, and the change-management approach to sustain adoption while reducing risk.

reportSections.title: Professional Conclusion (next steps + stakeholder communication)
reportSections.template: Close with concrete next steps for leadership and operations, plus the communication message each stakeholder group needs to support timely execution.

gapRules.prompt: Add at least one governance/control recommendation that includes a named owner and a clear implementation timeframe.
Name the single largest monitoring or retention blind spot and provide a concrete remediation path with expected risk-reduction effect.
State at least one operational or usability tradeoff created by your recommendation and how you will mitigate its negative effects.
Add a plain-language executive summary that clearly states the decision required, why it matters now, and the rationale in non-technical terms.
Explicitly justify impact and likelihood using scenario evidence, and show how that reasoning supports your selected risk level.
Provide explicit confidence rationale and describe what additional evidence would increase or decrease confidence.
